name: Ferruginous Hawk
sound_type: Alarm call -- loud rough descending kree-ah
ecosystem: Open grassland, shrub-steppe, rangeland. Eastern PNW Columbia Basin primary.
season: Resident eastern PNW; winter visitor western PNW lowlands
conservation: Least Concern (IUCN). Species of Conservation Concern (US). Threatened (Canada SARA).

vocalization.description: Loud, rough, descending kree-ah or kaah. Raspy texture, repeated 2-4 times. Nest defense context.
vocalization.character: Louder and rougher than Red-tailed Hawk scream; lower than Bald Eagle contact call
vocalization.function: Territorial defense, nest protection, alarm at intrusion

ecology.prey: Ground squirrels, prairie dogs, jackrabbits, pocket gophers
ecology.keystone_connection: Prairie dog colony abundance directly tracks Ferruginous Hawk productivity
ecology.hunting_technique: Soaring stoop, wind-hover, ground walking (rare among large raptors)
ecology.nesting: Trees, cliff ledges, ground on rocky outcrops when elevated sites unavailable

morphology.field_mark: Rust V-shaped leg feathers against white belly (light morph). White upper tail. Ferruginous upper parts.

connections.description: Alcohol Funnycar (E=7, grunge punk, Empty Records). E=13 combined. Both outsized in their domain.
Red-tailed Hawk (FIRST BUTEO). Ferruginous follows as largest buteo.
Rough-legged Hawk (Arctic buteo visitor, E=4). Third and final buteo in the engine's sequence.
Peregrine Falcon (FIRST FALCONIDAE). Major taxonomic boundary two degrees ahead.
